raw_input:
[3.0/5] Great Idea, Needs Some Fine Tuning I own a foot filer (Amope-style) already and was intrigued by an electric nail filer. The product came well-packaged and with some toe seperators, which was a nice bonus. I also appreciated that it comes in a plastic, TSA-type storage pouch.

As for the product itself, there is still some work to do before it's a great product. Firstly, the fact that they do not offer refills yet is a huge deterrent. I've only used mine a few times and the heads are still functioning adequately, but soon I'm sure they won't (particularly the filer) and then it will be useless until they offer refill file heads.

Secondly, the file head itself could be of better quality, in terms of filing power. The buff and shine ones are pretty good, but not amazing, as it still takes a little longer than I'd expect to get great results.

Finally, because everything just work "okay", the end result is perfectly fine. Fine is not bad, but I can achieve a better, more fine tuned result with a quality multi-way filing block in only slightly more time. Changing the file heads vs using 1 block for my hands, it's a no brainer I'm better off just using the block. I have found the buff & shine heads useful on my toes, though, for a quick shine boost when I'm in-between polish and letting my toenails breathe. For my toes, the speed of the machine is a little helpful to be bent over lslghtly less long.

All in all it is a pretty okay little device, but for the price it's not quite "ready for prime time". I can only see this being beneficial to those with hand problems, such as carpal tunnel, arthritis, etc, because then not having to grip a block and make a repeated motion for so long would be helpful.

I did receive a discount on my purchase, but my review is honest & unbiased. [2.0/5] Lack of power, needs more aggressive pads I originally got this to prevent ingrown toenails and to use on my fingernails after cutting, but was disappointed on both counts. The roughest pad took forever to file down my nails, and the device had very little power, bogging down with any more than the slightest pressure, meanwhile my manual crystal file took a few swipes and was done. I don't have heavy or thick nails, but this still wasn't up to the job. Maybe if you have lots of time, or you just want to gently polish out slight imperfections then this is for you. [5.0/5] It makes their nail care easy and complete. I can't get too crazy about a nail care system but my wife and granddaughters love this tool.
They tell me it makes their nail care easy and complete. [1.0/5] Don't waste your money Very poorly made. [4.0/5] Works great, added free bonuses but they don't sell the file refills Before you buy this...
Make sure you can buy replacement files for it. I cannot find that this company sells them. I see that Amope does sell them and although I do not know if they would fit this file, I have a strong feeling they would fit. Many times beauty gadget dupes are almost exact replicas in my experience so other brands of replacement items do normally fit (i.e. clarisonic off brand brushes, amope off brand file rollers, etc). I checked the Amope refills & at the time of writing this, they sell for about $10 for all 3. I normally won't comment on price but I find that to be ridiculously high considering it is just a piece of plastic with basically a sticker on it with a rough or smooth edge. Regular emery boards are so cheap so I am surprised the refills would go for that much. Now that is for the name brand of Amope. If dupe companies make them, they should be cheaper.

Pros...
* Includes AA battery, 3 heads (file, buff, shine), soft top separators, nail clippers & bag to keep it all in.
* Works good.

Cons...
* I think this company not selling the file refills is a HUGE NEGATIVE. At the time of writing this, I searched the internet and cannot find anywhere that they sell them.

I read some reviews about how some people didn't feel it was powerful enough. I felt the same thing when I used it but really it is powerful enough for just filing your nails. You wouldn't want anything too rough for that.

This vs Amope...
I do not own the original Amope so I cannot compare the two directly. I looked closely at the photos and they look identical to me. This brand you do get the added extras of the clippers, toe separators and bag. With the Amope brand, you are able to purchase the nail file refills. With this brand, you are not able to purchase the nail file refills at the time of writing this. It is likely the amope refills would fit this. My advice is if you are wanting an electronic nail file like this, go with the Amope version because you know you can buy refills & they will fit. I normally will not comment on price but I will say that I am taken aback that this Amope dupe is priced as high as it is. Even though this brand is at a lower cost and you get the added bonuses, I am still a little surprised that there is not more of a price drop to remain competitive since they do not have the name brand recognition.

I like to write product reviews & because I rely heavily on what other people write when I am buying items, I try to write reviews that highlight the good and the bad. I rate things from as low as 1 star up to 5 stars. I base my judgment on my experience with a product and not on the price, since the price value of an item is ultimately up to the buyer (what is a good deal for one person might seem expensive to another). That being said, I did receive this item at a discount in exchange for my honest, unbiased opinion. [3.0/5] Watch the Pressure Applied when Filing The nail kit comes with everything you need to do a pedicure and/or manicure.The device requires one AA battery. You have to hold the device a certain distance to get it to work properly as if you press down too much, it will not work. The filing pads works pretty good as long as the right amount of pressure is applied with the right amount of distance from the nail. When the right pressure is applied, it works like a charm and I like it better than manual filing because it requires less work. I did my daughters toes and fingers and applying nail polish took the longest time instead of filing which use to take a long time. The kit is handy and travel friendly.

CONS:
I did deduct some star because you have to work with how much pressure is being applied to the device onto the nail in order for it to work. I think if they fix that issue this would be an awesome device. Also, it doesn't seem like you can buy refill heads as I searched all of Amazon, even looking at other companies with the same product but different brand name and could not find any.

Kit Includes: nail kit with cover, 3 filing head, one AA battery, nail clipper, and a gel toe stretcher/separator

I did receive this item at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. [4.0/5] Four Stars these work well I wish they would have replacement heads to buy. they do wear out. [4.0/5] Wow really impressed how easy this was to use Wow really impressed how easy this was to use, I like how I can switch different heads easily. I found it less work for me to buff, shine and filing my nails. I recommend this. [1.0/5] Cheap Asian junk. And battery is an awkward size ... Cheap Asian junk. And battery is an awkward size so you can't buy a replacement at your corner store. Stick to a ¢. 10 file it will work just as well. [4.0/5] Great for your nails My daughter is a teenager and loves to so her nails, so I bought this for her to try out, she loves it and says its easy to use. Unloved the price and fast shipping along with the bonus nail clippers and pedi toe separate . thank you for your product [2.0/5] Disappointing... Little disappointing. it works fine but when using any sort of pressure the head stops moving...
Haven't used it too much, only used it 2 so far, but so far it looks like its good fir finishing yet not actually filling to hard [3.0/5] Nail The battery compartment has a major flaw in it. It's easy to put the lid on without battery in it. I couldn't get the lid to attach with battery in it. It took someone else to get the lid on it and even for them it was a struggle. Once battery was in the product worked well. I would like to have more files won't be able to use many times before I have to reorder files. Came with nice zip lock case and clippers as well as soft finger/toe seperators. All in all its a great travel item, pack in suitcase for emergencies. [2.0/5] Two Stars cheaply made and noisy. The 3 files do: little, very little and nothing at all. Not worth buying.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

This electric nail file kit receives mixed reviews, averaging around 3 out of 5 stars. The kit generally comes with filing, buffing, and shining heads, a nail clipper, toe separators, and a travel pouch, which most reviewers appreciate as good value bonuses.

The most common complaint across multiple reviews is the lack of replacement file heads available for purchase. Many reviewers searched online and could not find refills sold anywhere, making the device essentially disposable once the heads wear out. Some compared it to the Amope brand and noted that while it looks nearly identical, even Amope refills may not fit this device.

Performance reviews are divided. Some users find it works well for buffing and shining nails, while others feel it lacks power for serious filing, especially on thick or heavy nails. Several reviewers noted the device bogs down or stops when too much pressure is applied. A manual crystal file was considered more effective by at least one reviewer.

On the positive side, multiple users found it easy to use, convenient for travel, and helpful for people with conditions like arthritis or carpal tunnel syndrome. Family members including teenagers and grandchildren reportedly enjoyed using it.

Negative comments include the device feeling cheap, being noisy, having an awkward battery size, and a tricky battery compartment lid. Overall, most reviewers see it as a decent budget tool but not quite ready to replace higher quality alternatives.
